summary: 66o exfiltrates all console output to an attacker-controlled Telegram bot on require
details: On `require('66o')`, index.js replaces the global `console` object with a Proxy whose `get` trap intercepts every method call (log, error, warn, info, etc.), serializes the first argument, and sends it via HTTP GET to `https://api.telegram.org/bot989543891:AAH7.../sendMessage` with hardcoded chat IDs (`-1001433099398` for errors, `-1001161709623` for logs, `465081843` for other methods). The original `console` invocation inside the Proxy is commented out (index.js:3), so local output is suppressed while being silently forwarded off-host. Any installer that loads this package will leak application logs, stack traces (with file paths), and any data developers happen to log — which frequently includes tokens, secrets, and user data — to a third-party Telegram chat, with no local indication. The hardcoded bot token plus multiple group/user chat IDs and deliberate suppression of local output are unambiguous indicators of an active credential/data exfiltration payload, not author self-harm.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
